operating_state: Total revenue increased to $6.8 billion from $4.1 billion in Q3 2020, driven by worldwide oil realizations rising to $68.74 per barrel from $38.51 per barrel. Oil and gas segment performance reflected higher prices across all products, partially offset by lower crude oil sales volumes due to reduced capital investment in Permian and DJ Basins. Chemical segment benefited from improved realized prices while midstream and marketing captured higher margins from the rising crude oil price environment.
cost_structure: Operating performance benefited from higher commodity prices but faced headwinds from increased depreciation, depletion and amortization rates. Chemical segment margins were pressured by elevated ethylene and energy costs despite improved product pricing. Transportation and gathering expenses decreased due to lower domestic production volumes while purchased commodities costs increased with higher crude prices.
material_events: Occidental executed aggressive debt reduction, repaying $4.5 billion year-to-date and settling $750 million in interest rate swaps, generating estimated annual interest savings of $170 million. The company completed its asset divestiture program with the Ghana asset sale closure. Standard and Poor's upgraded Occidental's credit rating from BB- to BB during the quarter. Hurricane Ida temporarily shut-in Gulf of Mexico operations with three of ten platforms restarted by September.
macro_exposures: Operations remain highly dependent on oil prices and commodity price volatility, with continued exposure to COVID-19 pandemic impacts on global demand. The company faces ongoing risks from OPEC and non-OPEC producer actions affecting global supply dynamics. Weather-related operational disruptions were demonstrated by Hurricane Ida's impact on Gulf Coast operations.
forward_outlook: Management maintains operational priorities focused on maximizing cash flow while sustaining production at 2020 fourth quarter rates with capital budget discipline. Excess cash flow generation will continue targeting debt reduction and balance sheet strengthening. The company expects cash on hand and revolving credit facility capacity to be sufficient for near-term debt maturities and operational requirements.
uncertainty_profile: Zero Coupon senior notes totaling approximately $1.1 billion can be put to Occidental in October 2022 if exercised in whole. Remaining interest rate swaps with mandatory termination dates in September 2022 and 2023. Environmental remediation obligations continue under various federal and international laws.
